name: ASPro-PD is testing ambroxol in a phase 3 Parkinson disease trial without outcome results yet
summary: The phase 3 ASPro-PD program keeps lysosomal restoration clinically visible, but it is not efficacy evidence until results are available.
statement: The 2026 ASPro-PD protocol describes a 330-participant phase 3 randomized trial of high-dose ambroxol in genetically stratified Parkinson disease, with motor and non-motor progression outcomes plus GCase-related biomarker endpoints.
population_or_model: Parkinson disease patients with confirmed GBA1 status
time_horizon: Planned 104-week blinded treatment period plus open-label extension
caveats: This is a protocol and program-status finding, not an outcome result.
The population is Parkinson disease, not broad biological aging.
Phase 3 activity supports momentum only until clinical data are posted or published.
